note: All captions, hashtags, and visual directions are from the official spreadsheet. Do not modify.

posts.caption:
Peace is loud — once you've had the other thing. 🌅

People raised around chaos think peace is boring. It's not. It's the loudest thing in the world when you've spent years managing someone else's mood.

Comment PEACE if you've learned this too. ❤️

panels.scene_description: New balcony at dawn. Ivory chair empty. Red mug on small side table. Silver wind chime catching first light. Total quiet. No people.
panels.emotion: still morning, peace before the world wakes

panels.scene_description: Princess Kay on ivory blanket at feet of the chair. Alert and curious. Watching the silver wind chime move in the morning breeze. Red harness on.
Close-up of red mug held in hands — deep red nails wrapped around the warm ceramic. Morning steam rising. Balcony railing behind.
Silver wind chime close-up. Each ring catching soft morning light. Slight blur of breeze. Ivory chair edge visible. Quiet beauty.
panels.emotion: grace in the ordinary, peace made visible

panels.scene_description: Wide balcony scene. Princess Kay curled on ivory blanket. Red mug on table. Silver chime still. Morning light full. No other sound. No other presence.

posts.caption:
Red flags dressed in compliments. 🚩

I've heard this script before. Not from a realtor. From someone who broke me.

The pattern is the pattern, no matter who's running it.

Comment FLAG if you recognize it too. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Apartment interior during a showing. Empty rooms, hollow feeling. Afternoon light through uncovered windows. No warmth. Too much space.
Realtor's hand extending a business card with silver edging toward the camera. Professional, persistent. The card is elegant but unwanted.
Tannie's ivory sleeve and red-nailed hand gently declining the card — a polite push back. The card between two sets of hands. Refusal without drama.
Princess Kay in Tannie's arm, red harness visible. Looking at the business card with the side-eye of someone who recognizes a script they've heard before.
Business card resting on an ivory surface. Tannie's red-nailed hand leaving frame. The card left behind. Decision made. Princess Kay at the edge of the shot.

posts.caption:
The 5-piece wardrobe that built my confidence back. 👗

I stopped trying to be a different woman every day. I chose who I am and dressed accordingly.

Confidence is consistency.

Comment FIVE if you want the full breakdown of how to build yours. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Wide flatlay on ivory bedspread. Exactly 5 items arranged cleanly: a soft blouse, dark jeans, red sweater, silver hoop earrings, deep red heels. Clean, intentional, simple.
Red-nailed hand reaching into the flatlay, touching the red sweater. Making a choice. The rest of the items undisturbed. Deliberate.
Silver hoop earrings close-up on ivory fabric. Elegant and simple. Light catching the curve of each hoop. Nothing extra needed.
Deep red heels close-up on ivory bedspread. Rich color against the cream. Confident and grounded. The shoes of a woman who knows who she is.
Complete flatlay — all 5 items arranged. Red-nailed hands resting at the edge of the shot, done arranging. Princess Kay nosing one corner of the spread, curious.
panels.emotion: complete, at peace with the choice

posts.caption:
Y'all. I need your wisdom. 🚩

A — Say something gently
B — Stay out of it
C — Send her the red flag list anonymously

Comment your letter. Wednesday I'll tell you what I did and why.

This one matters. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Phone held in red-nailed hands. Screen showing a blurred text thread — readable shape but no legible words. Ivory coffee cup nearby. Still and tense.
Close-up of red nails scrolling the phone screen. The text thread long, blurred. Careful reading. The hand pauses. Deliberate attention.
Open notebook on an ivory surface. Silver pen. The letters A, B, C written out. Considering options. The coffee cup behind it, still steaming.
Ivory coffee cup, half-drunk. Steam still rising. Princess Kay sitting nearby, watching the situation with full attention. Quiet dilemma energy.
panels.emotion: pause, stillness in the middle of deciding

panels.scene_description: Phone face-down on ivory surface. Notebook with A-B-C still open beside it. Red pen marking something on the page. Princess Kay alert at the edge.
panels.emotion: decision pending, the choice is forming

posts.caption:
She gave me a LECTURE. In bark form. 🐕

Princess Kay does not believe in separation. We are working on it.

Also working on: accepting I'm her whole universe. Wild pressure. ❤️

Comment CLINGY if yours does this too.

panels.scene_description: Living room after someone has come home. Cushion knocked to floor. Ivory throw askew on the couch. Lone sock visible on rug. The room holds the evidence of dramatic protest.
Princess Kay mid-bark. Full dramatic energy. Mouth open, ears back. Expressing everything she has been holding since the door closed. Red harness on.
Silver collar tag jingling, catching light as Princess Kay shakes her head with emphasis. The tag in motion. The argument is making a point.
panels.emotion: punctuating the lecture, making herself heard

panels.scene_description: Lone sock on the floor. Princess Kay standing over it, staring at it accusingly as if it is evidence. The red harness visible. The case is being made.
Princess Kay sitting regally on the couch, red harness on, silver collar tag bright and still. Lecture concluded. The cushion back in place. Dignity fully restored.

posts.caption:
Telling the truth sometimes costs the thing you were trying to save. 🤍

She asked what I thought. I told her. It's been quiet since.

I'd rather lose the friendship than lose HER. She may not see it now. She will.

Comment TRUTH if you've made this call. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Ivory table. Phone face-down. Red mug, half-empty, still warm. Silver bracelet resting on the table edge. Quiet. The kind of quiet after something heavy.
Silver bracelet on a wrist, elbow resting on the ivory table. The weight of thinking. Head bent slightly. The mug nearby, cooling.
panels.emotion: deep contemplation, sitting with the weight

panels.scene_description: Princess Kay at her feet, looking upward with concern. Reading the mood completely. Not playful. Present. The red harness is on but she is very still.
panels.emotion: companionship in the quiet, love that stays

panels.scene_description: Close-up of the red mug, last bit of warmth. Red-nailed hands wrapped around it. Holding on to something warm when everything else is uncertain.
The phone still face-down on the ivory table. Princess Kay now on the chair beside her — red harness off, fully at rest. Staying. No answer has come. But she is not alone.
panels.emotion: waiting without despair, love that shows up

posts.caption:
The ones who rush you are the ones you walk from. 🚗

Dealer one lied about supply. Dealer two lied about value. Dealer three listened.

Going back to three.

Comment RUSH if you've learned that urgency is almost always a tactic. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Three car listing photos spread on an ivory surface. Laid out side by side. Red pen hovering above them. The decision has not been made yet.
Red pen crossing out the first car listing. Clean, decisive X. Silver thermos in background. The first one is gone.
Red pen crossing out the second listing. Firm. Silver thermos closer now, being reached for. The process is clear. This one too.
elements: red pen crossing out second listing

panels.scene_description: Red pen circling the third listing. The circle bold and clean. Ivory notebook open beside it with handwritten notes. The right choice becomes clear.
Clipboard holding the circled listing. Silver thermos to one side. Red pen resting across the notebook. Done. The decision was never about the car.

posts.caption:
I was her once. 🤍

Jesus found me somewhere I won't go back to. I don't preach from the front of the class. I preach from the back of it.

I'm the receipt. That's my ministry.

Comment RECEIPT if you've been the proof for someone else. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Ivory table in low evening light. Red Bible closed. Silver cross resting beside it. Phone nearby, screen blurred. Quiet and holy. Nothing urgent.
Red Bible open. Pages catching soft evening light. A red-nailed hand resting flat on the open page. Not searching. Resting.
panels.emotion: found, at rest in the word

panels.scene_description: Silver cross close-up on the ivory table. Low evening light catching the edges. The cross is simple, plain, silver. Holy in its ordinariness.
Phone held in red-nailed hands. Screen blurred but the shape of a long text is clear. Reading carefully. Something in it lands.
Red Bible closed again. Silver cross resting on top of it. Evening quiet. The testimony holds. The table holds both: the word and the cross.

posts.caption:
Paragliding today. 🪂

From up there, the old life looked TINY. Maybe it always was. Maybe I just couldn't see it from inside.

Elevation changes perspective. That's not just a metaphor.

Comment FLY if you need this reminder. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Wide aerial sky shot. Open blue in every direction. A few white clouds far below. Total freedom. The world not yet visible — just sky.
panels.emotion: pure possibility, before the fear falls away

panels.scene_description: Red helmet strap visible at the top of frame. The edge of an ivory jumpsuit. Wind in the fabric. Still climbing. The ground not visible yet.
Silver carabiners glinting in bright sunlight against the open sky. Sharp and strong. The only thing between flight and fall — and they hold.
Looking down from the paraglider. The land below small and far. The old life the size of a hand. Red helmet strap visible at the top of frame.
Wide sky again. Red helmet strap at the edge. Ivory jumpsuit sleeve. The full open sky. Wind. Everything below that once felt enormous: tiny.

posts.caption:
Tomorrow is an anniversary I don't celebrate anymore. 📅

My body still remembers the date. So I'm going to let it be tender. Not destroyed. Tender.

If the body remembers, the soul needs room.

Comment TENDER if you know a date like this. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Dim room. A wall calendar visible. One date circled in red. The circle deliberate, old. Low quiet light. No drama.
panels.emotion: the date exists, holding it quietly

panels.scene_description: Ivory Bible closed on a table. Silver cross beside it. Dim and still. Nothing opened yet. The Bible is there. That is enough.
Princess Kay curled at her feet. Red harness off, fully at rest. Sensing the weight without understanding it. Choosing to stay anyway.
Red-nailed hand resting on the Bible cover. Not opening it. Just resting a palm on the leather. Present with it. The cross visible nearby.
Calendar, Bible, silver cross, and Princess Kay — all in one quiet frame. Dim light. The date circled in red visible in the background. Weight without drama. Tender.

posts.caption:
Matching day. She is NOT thrilled. 🐕❤️

Princess Kay thinks I've betrayed her with a tiny sweater. I think it's the cutest thing I've ever owned.

She'll get over it. She always does.

Comment MATCH if you dress your dog up too. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Red sweater sleeves — just the arms. No face. No body above the shoulders. Ivory blanket behind. The sweater is deep red, soft. Cropped tight.
panels.emotion: the reveal, before we see her reaction

panels.scene_description: Princess Kay in a tiny matching red dog sweater. Expression: deeply unimpressed. Silver collar tag visible. The sweater fits. She is not grateful.
elements: Princess Kay in tiny red sweater

panels.scene_description: Silver collar tag close-up, catching light. The tiny red sweater visible around the neck. Very small. Very red. Very real.
Two red sweater cuffs side by side on ivory blanket. One human-sized sleeve end. One very small dog-sized sleeve end. Matching. Undeniable.
Princess Kay sitting on ivory blanket in the tiny red sweater. Silver collar bright. Expression: barely holding onto her dignity. The sweater will not be removed.
panels.emotion: acceptance, dignity intact by a thread

posts.caption:
She didn't leave my side. 🤍

I didn't tell her what today was. She knew. She always knows.

Dogs are prophets in fur. Rescued me as much as I rescued her.

Comment ANGEL if your dog has done this for you. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Dim bedroom. Ivory duvet on the bed. Silver cross necklace resting against the fabric. Low quiet light. No face. The room holds something heavy.
panels.emotion: the weight before the comfort arrives

panels.scene_description: Princess Kay curled on the ivory duvet. Red harness off. Fully at rest. Not playing. Not asking for anything. Simply present.
elements: Princess Kay curled on ivory duvet

panels.scene_description: Silver cross necklace close-up on the ivory duvet. Resting against the white fabric. Faith visible and still. The cross is plain and real.
panels.emotion: faith in the quiet, held without performance

panels.scene_description: Princess Kay curled on Tannie's chest. No face shown — only the silver cross necklace at the collarbone and the small dog above it. The dog chose this spot.
panels.emotion: the gift of being chosen, loved in the dark

panels.scene_description: Dim room. Ivory duvet. Silver cross. Princess Kay still curled in place. The whole scene quiet and complete. She didn't leave. She never left.
panels.emotion: held, not alone, the anniversary survived

posts.caption:
Mother's Day is complicated for a lot of us. 🌹

I wore red. Because I'm becoming the woman I needed. Because my mother did her best. Because God is the mother where moms fall short.

Happy Mother's Day to every woman parenting her own healing.

Comment HER if that's you. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Church interior seen from the back rows. Warm light filtering through high windows. Rows of wooden pews. The kind of quiet that holds history.
Ivory dress visible from behind, standing in the church aisle. Deep red heels on the stone floor. The stance is steady. She belongs here.
Silver cross necklace catching the warm church light. Seen from behind and slightly below. Simple and real against the ivory fabric.
Red carnation held in red-nailed hands. Bible resting on the pew beside it. Warm church light on the petals. Red on red — the color of becoming.
Wide shot from behind. Ivory dress. Deep red heels. Silver cross at neckline. Red carnation held at her side. Warm golden church light. A woman becoming the mother she needed.
elements: ivory dress from behind wide shot

posts.caption:
Week 3. 🕯️

Hard week. Honest week. I let the anniversary be tender. I told the truth to a friend. I found Jesus in the quiet again.

Still here. Still red-lipped. Still praying.

Comment STILLHERE. ❤️

panels.scene_description: Candle lit on a surface. Warm flame, steady. Ivory blanket folded nearby. Evening light soft. The beginning of the ending of a week.
Open journal on an ivory surface. Red pen mid-write, caught in motion. Silver cross beside the journal. Words being set down. The week going onto the page.
Princess Kay sleeping on the ivory blanket. Deep sleep. Fully at rest. The week has been long for both of them.
panels.emotion: rest earned, faithful companion at peace

panels.scene_description: Red pen resting across the open journal. Not writing anymore. Words on the page but not legible. Silver cross prominent beside it. Done.
Wide quiet scene. Candle burning low. Open journal. Red pen. Silver cross. Princess Kay sleeping. The whole week in one frame. Still here. Still burning.
